name: google-trends-mcp
description: Google Trends MCP with proxy rotation, auto-retry on Google blocks, and residential-proxy setup.
long_description:
Production-ready Google Trends MCP server with built-in defenses against Google's aggressive rate-limiting. Designed for keyword research and SEO work in long agent sessions where naïve Google Trends clients hit 429 after a few queries.

What you get via the agent:
• Trends API — `compare_keywords`, `get_interest_by_region`, `get_related_queries` for any term.
• Proxy rotation — feed it a list of residential proxies (Webshare, IPRoyal, Smartproxy formats supported) and the server rotates per request, drops dead ones, and exposes a `proxy_refresh` tool to re-load the pool.
• Auto-retry on block — Google's 429 / consent-page traps are retried on the next proxy automatically.
• Direct mode — set `PROXIES_ENABLED=false` to skip the proxy layer entirely (small queries from a clean IP).

Proxies are configured via env vars (`PROXY_URL` single endpoint, `PROXY_LIST` comma-separated, or `PROXY_LIST_FILE` for a file with one URL per line). See the README for proxy provider recommendations, format examples, and the OSS → hosted → x402 roadmap.

proxy_url.title: Single rotating proxy endpoint
proxy_url.description: e.g. http://user:pass@gate.smartproxy.com:7000 — the provider handles IP rotation.

proxy_list.title: Comma-separated proxy list
proxy_list.description: http://user:pass@host:port,http://user2:pass2@host2:port2,… — validated on startup, dead ones auto-dropped.

proxy_list_file.title: Path to a proxy-list file
proxy_list_file.description: File with one proxy URL per line (# comments allowed). Re-read on `proxy_refresh` tool call.

proxies_enabled.title: Enable proxy layer
proxies_enabled.description: Set to "false" to skip the proxy logic and use direct requests. Default: enabled.
